name: struck-bell
description: A struck resonant body built from the exciter -> resonator pair: an `impact` lights up a `modal` bank of damped partials. No oscillator can voice a bell cleanly; modes can. Replay with: tono replay docs/examples/struck-bell.json

steps.note: The impact is a unit-area force pulse: `velocity` sets the energy, `hardness` (0.85) the brightness — how far up the bank it reaches. Each mode is a damped sine; near-harmonic ratios + a long fundamental decay read as 'bell'. Stretch the decays for a church bell, shrink them for a glass ping; nudge the ratios off-harmonic for metal. `engine: 1` is set so the new kernels apply (here it gates nothing audible — modal is new — but it is what every engine-1 sound carries).
A bright two-tone coin/UI ding: a hard strike into three high, fast-decaying modes. The 3:2-ish top interval is the recognizable 'pickup' shimmer.
